steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause: “Nora sought to increase Latina representation in corporate [BLANK].” The right side is also an independent clause: “[BLANK] founded Las Comadres para las Americas, an international community that for over two decades has served as a resource and information network for Latina business professionals.”

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: A period separates two independent clauses into two sentences. Choice D uses a period after “settings,” correctly separating the two sentences. Choices A, B, and C create punctuation errors: A leaves no punctuation (fused sentence), B uses only a comma (comma splice), and C uses “and” without the conventional comma. This matches choice D — D is correct.
